zh.lifeSummary: 當Eleanor de Clinton於1250年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾阿敏頓時，她的父親Thomas Clinton20歲，母親Maud Bracebridge16歲。她於1270年與Nicholas de Stafford在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1287年8月22日在英國斯塔福爾斯塔福德享年37歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clinton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Louth, Westmeath) and Scottish: shortened form of   McClinton  .

English: habitational name, either from Glympton in Oxfordshire, named as ‘settlement (Old English tūn) on the Glym river’, a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright stream’, or from Glinton in Northamptonshire, early recorded as Clinton (named with an unrecorded Old English element akin to Middle Low German glinde ‘enclosure, fence’ + Old English tūn). The surname was taken to Ireland in or before the 16th century.

History: Charles Clinton (born 1690 in Longford, Ireland) organized a group of colonists and founded the settlement of Little Britain, Ulster County, NY, in 1731. His son George Clinton (1739–1812) was governor of NY (1777–95), and they had many prominent descendants. — An English aristocratic family called Clinton was founded by Geoffrey de Clinton (flourished 1130), who held lands at Glympton, Oxfordshire. He was chamberlain and treasurer to Henry I (1100–35).

variants.name: McClinton
meanings: Irish (northern) and Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Fhionntain (Scottish), Mac Giolla Fhionntáin (Irish) ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Fionntan’, whose name has been explained as ‘the white ancient’.

nameMeanings.name: Eleanor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

variants.name: Ellenor
meanings: Variant spelling of   Eleanor  , the result of blending with   Ellen .

variants.name: Elenor
meanings: From an Old French respelling of the Old Provençal name Alienor. This has sometimes been taken as a derivative of Helen , but it is more probably of Germanic derivation (the first element being ali ‘other, foreign’ the second is obscure). The name was introduced to England by Eleanor of Aquitaine ( 1122–1204 ), who came from Aquitaine in south-west France to be the wife of King Henry II . It was also borne by Eleanor of Provence, the wife of Henry III , and Eleanor of Castile, wife of Edward I .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Eleanor de Clinton was born in 1250, in Amington, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Clinton, was 20 and her mother, Maud Bracebridge, was 16. She married Nicholas de Stafford in 1270, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 22 August 1287, in Stafford, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 37.

ja.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Clintonが1250年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアアミントンで生まれた時，父Thomas Clintonは20歳，母Maud Bracebridgeは16歳であった。1270年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードでNicholas de Staffordと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1287年8月22日にイギリススタフォードシュアイアスタフォードで37歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Eleanor de Clinton(이)가 1250년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 아밍턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Clinton(은)는 20세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Bracebridge(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1270년 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 Nicholas de Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1287년 8월 22일 영국 스태퍼드실르 스태퍼드에서 37세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Eleanor de Clinton родилась в 1250 г. в местности амингтон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Clinton было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Bracebridge, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Nicholas de Stafford в 1270 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 22 августа 1287 г. в местности стаффорд, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 37 лет.
